methodology: Grep counts via Claude Grep tool against ship/web/src, ship/api/src, ship/shared/src. tsc verified via 'pnpm type-check' which runs --noEmit across all 3 workspaces. ESLint configuration checked via 'pnpm lint' (none configured).

strict_mode.note: tsc --noEmit passes cleanly on shared/, web/, and api/. The plan's strict-mode-explosion contingency does not apply — strict is already enforced. Type safety violations come only from explicit any/as/!/ts-ignore patterns.

any_usage_broad.note: Catches all forms — explicit annotations, <any>, any[], Record<string, any>, function params, etc.

type_assertions_as.note: Counts ' as TypeName' assertions where target is a capitalized type. Excludes ' as const' and lowercased keywords.

non_null_assertions.note: Counts '!.\w' (non-null assertion before property access). Does NOT count '!' negation operator.

ts_ignore_directives.note: Only 1 occurrence across the entire codebase (web/src/components/icons/uswds/Icon.test.tsx). Exceptionally clean.

conservative.note: Uses : any (explicit annotation) only. Used as primary baseline.

broad.note: Uses \bany\b (catches all any forms). Higher count includes <any>, any[], Record<string, any>, etc.

top_violation_dense_files_production_cod.note: The single largest type-safety hotspot. Likely high-leverage target for U11.
Route handler with high type debt.
Yjs binary conversion helpers — type erasure is common here.
Heavy route handler — week documents are complex.
Dynamic property rendering — common any/as source.

top_violation_dense_files_test_code.note: Test file. Many as assertions are legitimate (mock typing). Lower priority for fixing.

type_coverage.interpretation: 93.47% of identifiers have a non-any type. The remaining 6.53% (11,547 identifiers) is primarily implicit any from type inference — far more than the ~400 explicit grep violations. This is a strong baseline; comparable codebases of this size often sit in 75-88%.
type_coverage.stretch_target: 94.00%+ after improvements (~1000 identifiers shifted from any to a real type)

eslint.note: No ESLint configuration in the Ship codebase. See eslint-baseline.json for full details.

audit_implications: Strict mode is already aggressively enforced — there is no 'turn strict on' lever for the 25% improvement target.
Improvement strategy should target the top 5 production-code violation-dense files first for highest ROI.
Test files account for a substantial portion of `as` assertions; these are usually legitimate test idioms and lower priority.
Total target of ~100 violations fixed (conservative count) is achievable by addressing 2-3 of the top files.
ESLint addition would catch additional quality issues beyond TypeScript's reach and is a recommended improvement (not part of PDF target).
Type-coverage percentage metric still pending — to be filled in once 'pnpm dlx type-coverage --detail' runs.
